title: Klarna Group issues senior unsecured bond 2017
full_text:
Klarna Bank AB (publ) has issued a 3-year senior unsecured bond in the amount of SEK 2 billion

Klarna has issued a senior unsecured bond of SEK 2 billion with a floating rate coupon of 3 months Stibor + 1.45 percent and a final maturity in September 2020. This supports Klarna’s continued growth.

Nordea Markets and Swedbank acted as bookrunners in the transaction.

For more information, please visit www.klarna.com or contact press@klarna.com

About the Klarna Group

Klarna is one of Europe’s largest banks and offers easy payment solutions for 80 million consumers and over 190,000 merchants. The company is now active in 18 markets with over 1600 employees. Klarna was founded in 2005 in Sweden with the idea of simplifying shopping. The core business of the company are pay after delivery, direct debit and installment payments. Today, Klarna is one of the fastest growing companies in Europe.
